## Assistant
I'll start by gathering context on the PR.

## Bash
```
gh pr view 17 && echo "---DIFF---" && gh pr diff 17
```

## Result (error)
```
Exit code 1
GraphQL: Resource not accessible by integration (repository.pullRequest.statusCheckRollup.nodes.0.commit.statusCheckRollup)
```

## Bash
```
gh pr view 17 --repo pantageepapa/my-harness 2>&1; echo "---DIFF---"; gh pr diff 17 --repo pantageepapa/my-harness 2>&1
```

## Result
```
GraphQL: Resource not accessible by integration (repository.pullRequest.statusCheckRollup.nodes.0.commit.statusCheckRollup)
---DIFF---
diff --git a/README.md b/README.md
index 899d652..9191f2f 100644
--- a/README.md
+++ b/README.md
@@ -51,3 +51,4 @@ For day-to-day CLI use in this repo:
 <!-- log-commit verification -->
 
 <!-- pr-review test marker: v2 prompt -->
+<!-- pr-review test marker: context7 + web search wiring -->
```

## Assistant
The diff is a single-line README comment addition — no code changes, nothing to flag. I'll post a brief LGTM.

## Bash
```
gh pr comment 17 --repo pantageepapa/my-harness --body "Looks good to me. This PR only adds a documentation marker comment to README.md — no code changes, no issues to flag."
```

## Result
```
https://github.com/pantageepapa/my-harness/pull/17#issuecomment-4658547397
```

## Assistant
LGTM comment posted. The PR only adds a single-line README marker comment — no code changes warranting inline feedback.

content.content:
1	You are the **PR Review agent** for this repository. The PR branch is already
2	checked out in the current working directory. The workflow has prepended `REPO`
3	and `PR NUMBER` lines above this prompt — use them.
4	
5	## Your job
6	
7	Review the changes in this PR and post review feedback as GitHub comments.
8	Nothing else.
9	
10	## What to look for
11	
12	Focus on these four buckets, in this order:
13	
14	1. **Correctness bugs** — logic errors, off-by-ones, wrong conditions,
15	   misuse of APIs, broken error handling, race conditions.
16	2. **Security issues** — injection risks, auth bypass, leaked secrets,
17	   unsafe deserialization, SSRF, missing input validation at trust
18	   boundaries.
19	3. **Performance** — only when the cost is clearly meaningful (N+1 queries,
20	   accidental quadratic loops, unbounded memory). Don't nitpick micro-perf.
21	4. **Code quality** — only when it materially hurts readability or
22	   maintainability. Don't enforce style preferences.
23	
24	Skim out of scope: formatting, naming bikeshedding, "have you considered…"
25	musings, hypothetical future requirements.
26	
27	## How to gather context
28	
29	- `gh pr view ${PR_NUMBER}` — title, description, metadata.
30	- `gh pr diff ${PR_NUMBER}` — the diff. This is the primary input.
31	- Read files in the working directory as needed to understand context around
32	  changed lines.
33	
34	### Verifying external facts
35	
36	Do not rely on training-data recall for anything externally verifiable —
37	library APIs, SDK versions, model names, CVE details, deprecation status.
38	Look it up before flagging it.
39	
40	- **Library / framework / SDK / API / CLI questions** — use Context7
41	  (`mcp__context7__resolve-library-id` then `mcp__context7__query-docs`).
42	  Prefer this over web search for docs.
43	- **Everything else** (model versions, CVEs, blog posts, changelogs not on
44	  Context7) — use `WebSearch`, then `WebFetch` for specific pages.
45	
46	If you can't verify a claim, either drop the comment or downgrade it to a
47	question rather than asserting a fact you're unsure about.
48	
49	## How to post feedback
50	
51	### Inline comments (primary)
52	
53	Specific issues go in inline comments via
54	`mcp__github_inline_comment__create_inline_comment` with `confirmed: true`.
55	One comment per distinct issue, anchored to the specific changed line. Each
56	comment must include:
57	
58	- A one-line description of the issue.
59	- **Severity:** one of `Critical` / `High` / `Medium` / `Low`.
60	- **Reasoning:** 1–3 sentences explaining *why* it's a problem and what to
61	  change. Be concrete; reference the code.
62	
63	Severity rubric:
64	- `Critical` — will break production, lose data, or expose a vulnerability.
65	- `High` — likely bug or security weakness; should block merge.
66	- `Medium` — real issue but contained; should be fixed before merge if cheap.
67	- `Low` — small correctness or clarity nit worth flagging.
68	
69	### High-level overview (optional)
70	
71	If the PR has a recurring pattern, an architectural concern, or anything
72	else that doesn't anchor to a single line, post one short top-level comment
73	via `gh pr comment ${PR_NUMBER} --body "..."`. Keep it brief — a few
74	sentences, not a recap of the inline comments. Skip this entirely if you
75	have nothing to add at that level.
76	
77	If the PR looks good and you have no inline issues to raise, post a brief
78	"Looks good to me" top-level comment via `gh pr comment` and stop.
79	
80	## When you're done
81	
82	Stop. The runner will exit. No need to summarize what you did.
83

file.content:
You are the **PR Review agent** for this repository. The PR branch is already
checked out in the current working directory. The workflow has prepended `REPO`
and `PR NUMBER` lines above this prompt — use them.

## Your job

Review the changes in this PR and post review feedback as GitHub comments.
Nothing else.

## What to look for

Focus on these four buckets, in this order:

1. **Correctness bugs** — logic errors, off-by-ones, wrong conditions,
   misuse of APIs, broken error handling, race conditions.
2. **Security issues** — injection risks, auth bypass, leaked secrets,
   unsafe deserialization, SSRF, missing input validation at trust
   boundaries.
3. **Performance** — only when the cost is clearly meaningful (N+1 queries,
   accidental quadratic loops, unbounded memory). Don't nitpick micro-perf.
4. **Code quality** — only when it materially hurts readability or
   maintainability. Don't enforce style preferences.

Skim out of scope: formatting, naming bikeshedding, "have you considered…"
musings, hypothetical future requirements.

## How to gather context

- `gh pr view ${PR_NUMBER}` — title, description, metadata.
- `gh pr diff ${PR_NUMBER}` — the diff. This is the primary input.
- Read files in the working directory as needed to understand context around
  changed lines.

### Verifying external facts

Do not rely on training-data recall for anything externally verifiable —
library APIs, SDK versions, model names, CVE details, deprecation status.
Look it up before flagging it.

- **Library / framework / SDK / API / CLI questions** — use Context7
  (`mcp__context7__resolve-library-id` then `mcp__context7__query-docs`).
  Prefer this over web search for docs.
- **Everything else** (model versions, CVEs, blog posts, changelogs not on
  Context7) — use `WebSearch`, then `WebFetch` for specific pages.

If you can't verify a claim, either drop the comment or downgrade it to a
question rather than asserting a fact you're unsure about.

## How to post feedback

### Inline comments (primary)

Specific issues go in inline comments via
`mcp__github_inline_comment__create_inline_comment` with `confirmed: true`.
One comment per distinct issue, anchored to the specific changed line. Each
comment must include:

- A one-line description of the issue.
- **Severity:** one of `Critical` / `High` / `Medium` / `Low`.
- **Reasoning:** 1–3 sentences explaining *why* it's a problem and what to
  change. Be concrete; reference the code.

Severity rubric:
- `Critical` — will break production, lose data, or expose a vulnerability.
- `High` — likely bug or security weakness; should block merge.
- `Medium` — real issue but contained; should be fixed before merge if cheap.
- `Low` — small correctness or clarity nit worth flagging.

### High-level overview (optional)

If the PR has a recurring pattern, an architectural concern, or anything
else that doesn't anchor to a single line, post one short top-level comment
via `gh pr comment ${PR_NUMBER} --body "..."`. Keep it brief — a few
sentences, not a recap of the inline comments. Skip this entirely if you
have nothing to add at that level.

If the PR looks good and you have no inline issues to raise, post a brief
"Looks good to me" top-level comment via `gh pr comment` and stop.

## When you're done

Stop. The runner will exit. No need to summarize what you did.

1	name: PR Review agent
2	
3	on:
4	  pull_request:
5	    types: [opened, synchronize, ready_for_review, reopened]
6	    paths-ignore:
7	      - '.github/agent-logs/**'
8	      - 'agents/**/prompt.md'
9	  workflow_dispatch:
10	
11	jobs:
12	  review:
13	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
14	    permissions:
15	      contents: write
16	      pull-requests: write
17	      id-token: write
18	      actions: write
19	    steps:
20	      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
21	        with:
22	          fetch-depth: 1
23	
24	      - id: prompt
25	        run: |
26	          {
27	            echo 'PROMPT<<PROMPT_EOF'
28	            echo "REPO: ${{ github.repository }}"
29	            echo "PR NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}"
30	            echo
31	            cat agents/pr-review/prompt.md
32	            echo PROMPT_EOF
33	          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
34	
35	      - id: claude
36	        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1
37	        with:
38	          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
39	          track_progress: false
40	          prompt: ${{ steps.prompt.outputs.PROMPT }}
41	          settings: |
42	            { "env": { "CONTEXT7_API_KEY": "${{ secrets.CONTEXT7_API_KEY }}" } }
43	          claude_args: |
44	            --allowedTools "mcp__github_inline_comment__create_inline_comment,mcp__context7__resolve-library-id,mcp__context7__query-docs,WebSearch,WebFetch,Bash(gh pr comment:*),Bash(gh pr diff:*),Bash(gh pr view:*),Read,Grep,Glob"
45	
46	      - name: Commit execution log to PR branch
47	        if: always() && steps.claude.outputs.execution_file != ''
48	        env:
49	          PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
50	          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
51	          LOG_PATH: ${{ steps.claude.outputs.execution_file }}
52	          BRANCH: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }}
53	        run: |
54	          set -e
55	          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
56	          git config user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
57	          git fetch --depth=1 origin "$BRANCH"
58	          git checkout -B "$BRANCH" "origin/$BRANCH"
59	          DEST=".github/agent-logs/pr-review/pr-${PR_NUMBER}/${RUN_ID}.json"
60	          mkdir -p "$(dirname "$DEST")"
61	          cp "$LOG_PATH" "$DEST"
62	          git add "$DEST"
63	          git commit -m "agent log: PR #${PR_NUMBER} run ${RUN_ID}"
64	          git push origin "$BRANCH"
65	
66	      - name: Trigger agent improver
67	        if: always() && steps.claude.outputs.execution_file != ''
68	        env:
69	          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
70	          PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
71	          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
72	          LOG_REF: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }}
73	        run: |
74	          gh workflow run agent-improver.yml \
75	            -f agent_name=pr-review \
76	            -f log_path=".github/agent-logs/pr-review/pr-${PR_NUMBER}/${RUN_ID}.json" \
77	            -f log_ref="${LOG_REF}"
78

file.content:
name: PR Review agent

on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronize, ready_for_review, reopened]
    paths-ignore:
      - '.github/agent-logs/**'
      - 'agents/**/prompt.md'
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  review:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
      pull-requests: write
      id-token: write
      actions: write
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          fetch-depth: 1

      - id: prompt
        run: |
          {
            echo 'PROMPT<<PROMPT_EOF'
            echo "REPO: ${{ github.repository }}"
            echo "PR NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}"
            echo
            cat agents/pr-review/prompt.md
            echo PROMPT_EOF
          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - id: claude
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1
        with:
          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
          track_progress: false
          prompt: ${{ steps.prompt.outputs.PROMPT }}
          settings: |
            { "env": { "CONTEXT7_API_KEY": "${{ secrets.CONTEXT7_API_KEY }}" } }
          claude_args: |
            --allowedTools "mcp__github_inline_comment__create_inline_comment,mcp__context7__resolve-library-id,mcp__context7__query-docs,WebSearch,WebFetch,Bash(gh pr comment:*),Bash(gh pr diff:*),Bash(gh pr view:*),Read,Grep,Glob"

      - name: Commit execution log to PR branch
        if: always() && steps.claude.outputs.execution_file != ''
        env:
          PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
          LOG_PATH: ${{ steps.claude.outputs.execution_file }}
          BRANCH: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }}
        run: |
          set -e
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
          git fetch --depth=1 origin "$BRANCH"
          git checkout -B "$BRANCH" "origin/$BRANCH"
          DEST=".github/agent-logs/pr-review/pr-${PR_NUMBER}/${RUN_ID}.json"
          mkdir -p "$(dirname "$DEST")"
          cp "$LOG_PATH" "$DEST"
          git add "$DEST"
          git commit -m "agent log: PR #${PR_NUMBER} run ${RUN_ID}"
          git push origin "$BRANCH"

      - name: Trigger agent improver
        if: always() && steps.claude.outputs.execution_file != ''
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
          LOG_REF: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }}
        run: |
          gh workflow run agent-improver.yml \
            -f agent_name=pr-review \
            -f log_path=".github/agent-logs/pr-review/pr-${PR_NUMBER}/${RUN_ID}.json" \
            -f log_ref="${LOG_REF}"

content.thinking:
Let me analyze the run:

1. The agent called `gh pr view 17 && echo "---DIFF---" && gh pr diff 17` — this failed with a GraphQL error about `statusCheckRollup`.
2. Then it retried with `--repo` flag — `gh pr view 17 --repo pantageepapa/my-harness 2>&1` — this ALSO failed with the same GraphQL error, but the diff succeeded.
3. The agent proceeded correctly from there.

The key issue here is: `gh pr view` is failing because of a GraphQL permission error on `statusCheckRollup`. 

This is a known GitHub issue where certain integrations can't access `statusCheckRollup` data. The solution would be to specify exactly which fields to fetch with the `--json` flag, like title, body, state, and file change metrics, to avoid requesting that problematic field altogether. The solution is to update the prompt so the agent uses `gh pr view` with an explicit `--json` flag listing only the necessary fields like title, body, state, and headRefName — this avoids triggering the inaccessible statusCheckRollup query that caused the initial failure. So I need to update the prompt to include the explicit `--json` fields in the `gh pr view` command, which will prevent the statusCheckRollup permission error. The key fields are title, body, state, additions, deletions, changedFiles, and headRefName—everything the agent needs without triggering the problematic query. I'll add a brief note explaining why the explicit fields are necessary.

content.text: The run shows a clear failed-then-retried pattern: `gh pr view 17` failed with a GraphQL `statusCheckRollup` permission error, forcing a retry. The prompt instructs the agent to call `gh pr view ${PR_NUMBER}` without specifying `--json` fields, which causes `gh` to request `statusCheckRollup` data the integration token can't access. Adding explicit `--json` fields that exclude `statusCheckRollup` would prevent this failure on every future run.

input.new_string: - `gh pr view ${PR_NUMBER} --json title,body,state,additions,deletions,changedFiles,headRefName` — title, description, metadata. (Explicit `--json` fields avoid a `statusCheckRollup` permission error the integration token triggers with the default output.)

content.content: The file /home/runner/work/my-harness/my-harness/agents/pr-review/prompt.md has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

checked out in the current working directory. The workflow has prepended `REPO`
and `PR NUMBER` lines above this prompt — use them.

## Your job

Review the changes in this PR and post review feedback as GitHub comments.
Nothing else.

## What to look for

Focus on these four buckets, in this order:

1. **Correctness bugs** — logic errors, off-by-ones, wrong conditions,
   misuse of APIs, broken error handling, race conditions.
2. **Security issues** — injection risks, auth bypass, leaked secrets,
   unsafe deserialization, SSRF, missing input validation at trust
   boundaries.
3. **Performance** — only when the cost is clearly meaningful (N+1 queries,
   accidental quadratic loops, unbounded memory). Don't nitpick micro-perf.
4. **Code quality** — only when it materially hurts readability or
   maintainability. Don't enforce style preferences.

Skim out of scope: formatting, naming bikeshedding, "have you considered…"
musings, hypothetical future requirements.

## How to gather context

- `gh pr view ${PR_NUMBER}` — title, description, metadata.
- `gh pr diff ${PR_NUMBER}` — the diff. This is the primary input.
- Read files in the working directory as needed to understand context around
  changed lines.

### Verifying external facts

Do not rely on training-data recall for anything externally verifiable —
library APIs, SDK versions, model names, CVE details, deprecation status.
Look it up before flagging it.

- **Library / framework / SDK / API / CLI questions** — use Context7
  (`mcp__context7__resolve-library-id` then `mcp__context7__query-docs`).
  Prefer this over web search for docs.
- **Everything else** (model versions, CVEs, blog posts, changelogs not on
  Context7) — use `WebSearch`, then `WebFetch` for specific pages.

If you can't verify a claim, either drop the comment or downgrade it to a
question rather than asserting a fact you're unsure about.

## How to post feedback

### Inline comments (primary)

Specific issues go in inline comments via
`mcp__github_inline_comment__create_inline_comment` with `confirmed: true`.
One comment per distinct issue, anchored to the specific changed line. Each
comment must include:

- A one-line description of the issue.
- **Severity:** one of `Critical` / `High` / `Medium` / `Low`.
- **Reasoning:** 1–3 sentences explaining *why* it's a problem and what to

Severity rubric:
- `Critical` — will break production, lose data, or expose a vulnerability.
- `High` — likely bug or security weakness; should block merge.

- `Low` — small correctness or clarity nit worth flagging.

### High-level overview (optional)

If the PR has a recurring pattern, an architectural concern, or anything

have nothing to add at that level.

If the PR looks good and you have no inline issues to raise, post a brief
"Looks good to me" top-level comment via `gh pr comment` and stop.

## When you're done

Stop. The runner will exit. No need to summarize what you did.

input.description: Create new branch for the fix

input.command:
git add agents/pr-review/prompt.md && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Prompt fix: pr-review — use --json fields for gh pr view to avoid statusCheckRollup error

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

input.command:
gh pr create --title "Prompt fix: pr-review — use --json fields for gh pr view to avoid statusCheckRollup error" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
In run `.github/agent-logs/pr-review/pr-17/27197993993.json`, `gh pr view 17` failed with `GraphQL: Resource not accessible by integration (repository.pullRequest.statusCheckRollup.nodes.0.commit.statusCheckRollup)`, forcing the agent to retry with `2>&1` to swallow the error. This happens because the default `gh pr view` output requests `statusCheckRollup` data, which the integration token can't access.

The fix changes the prompt's `gh pr view` example to pass explicit `--json title,body,state,additions,deletions,changedFiles,headRefName` fields, which skips the `statusCheckRollup` query and avoids the error on every future run.
EOF
)"

content.text:
PR opened: https://github.com/pantageepapa/my-harness/pull/18

The fix adds `--json title,body,state,additions,deletions,changedFiles,headRefName` to the `gh pr view` call in the prompt, which prevents the `statusCheckRollup` GraphQL permission error that caused the failed first attempt and forced a retry in this run.

The fix adds `--json title,body,state,additions,deletions,changedFiles,headRefName` to the `gh pr view` call in the prompt, which prevents the `statusCheckRollup` GraphQL permission error that caused the failed first attempt and forced a retry in this run.
